articles.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targeting Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and developing export infrastructure.
• Phase II aims to further incentivize domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and boost green hydrogen production capacity.
• The mission seeks to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production, utilization, and export.
• Emphasizes public-private partnerships and research & development to reduce production costs.
• This phase is crucial for achieving India's energy independence and climate targets by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Energy; Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) launched in 2023.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Phase II focuses on scaling up production and developing export infrastructure.
Target to produce 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) of green hydrogen by 2030.
SIGHT program (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) supports domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and production of green hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's energy security strategy through green hydrogen.
Analyze the economic potential for job creation, industrial growth, and export revenue.
Examine the environmental benefits in reducing carbon emissions and achieving climate targets.
Evaluate challenges like high initial costs, technology scaling, and infrastructure development.
Role of NGHM in Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative and global energy transition.
articles.imageDescription: An artist's rendition of a large-scale green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making its production process carbon-free and sustainable.

articles.headline: 11th International Yoga Day Celebrated with Theme 'Yoga for Self and Society'
articles.explainer:
• India spearheaded global celebrations for the 11th International Yoga Day, with events held nationwide and internationally.
• The theme for 2025, 'Yoga for Self and Society,' emphasized yoga's role in individual well-being and community harmony.
• Prime Minister led the main event in a major city, advocating for wider adoption of yoga practices for physical and mental health.
• Ministry of AYUSH organized numerous workshops, camps, and online sessions to promote yoga benefits.
• Millions participated, showcasing yoga's growing global appeal and India's cultural soft power.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
prelimsPoints: International Yoga Day is celebrated annually on June 21st.
First observed in 2015, following a UN resolution proposed by India.
The theme for International Yoga Day 2025 is 'Yoga for Self and Society'.
Ministry of AYUSH is the nodal ministry for promotion of Yoga in India.
The date June 21st marks the Summer Solstice in the Northern Hemisphere, considered significant in yogic tradition.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of International Yoga Day in promoting India's soft power and cultural diplomacy.
Analyze the role of yoga in addressing modern lifestyle diseases and promoting holistic health.
Evaluate government initiatives like the Ministry of AYUSH in integrating traditional wellness practices into public health policies.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in making yoga accessible to all sections of society globally.
How does yoga contribute to individual well-being and societal harmony as per the 2025 theme?
articles.imageDescription: A large crowd performing yoga asanas in an open ground, with a stage and banners in the background.
articles.trivia: The word 'Yoga' is derived from the Sanskrit root 'Yuj', meaning 'to join' or 'to unite', symbolizing the union of individual consciousness with universal consciousness.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest meeting.
• The decision was influenced by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food and energy sectors.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to bringing inflation within the target band while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the central bank, with future rate adjustments dependent on inflation trajectory.
• The MPC also highlighted global economic uncertainties and geopolitical developments impacting domestic inflation outlook.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy and its instruments.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
The primary objective of monetary policy in India is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2% (2% to 6%).
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Reverse repo rate is the rate at which the RBI borrows money from commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global scenario.
Analyze the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflationary pressures in India.
Examine the impact of global geopolitical events and supply chain disruptions on India's domestic inflation.
Evaluate the role of the MPC in ensuring transparency and accountability in monetary policy decisions.
Suggest alternative or complementary measures to monetary policy for sustainable economic stability.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai with its distinctive logo, under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: The first Governor of the Reserve Bank of India was Sir Osborne Smith, while the first Indian Governor was C.D. Deshmukh.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
• The ruling clarified provisions related to cross-border data transfers and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• Petitioners had challenged certain clauses citing concerns over privacy, surveillance, and exemptions for government agencies.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security.
• This judgment provides much-needed legal clarity for businesses and individuals on data handling practices.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Cyber security basics; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data.
It recognizes the 'right to privacy' as a fundamental right (Puttaswamy judgment).
The Act establishes a Data Protection Board of India for enforcement.
Key principles include consent, data minimization, and purpose limitation.
The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data within India and to certain offshore processing activities.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on India's digital economy and individual privacy.
Discuss the balance struck by the DPDP Act between state surveillance needs and fundamental right to privacy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Data Protection Board in ensuring compliance and grievance redressal.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust data protection framework in a digitally evolving society.
Compare India's data protection law with global standards like GDPR and its impact on international trade and data flows.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi, with legal documents and a gavel in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The landmark K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India judgment in 2017 declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Data Protection

articles.headline: India-ASEAN FTA Review Concludes, Paving Way for Enhanced Trade and Investment
articles.explainer:
• India and the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) successfully concluded the review of their Free Trade Agreement (FTA).
• The review aimed to address existing trade imbalances, simplify rules of origin, and expand the scope of the agreement.
• Negotiations focused on reducing non-tariff barriers and promoting greater integration of supply chains.
• Both sides expressed optimism about the potential for significant growth in bilateral trade and investment post-review.
• The revised FTA is expected to come into effect by early 2026, boosting regional economic cooperation.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization comprising ten Southeast Asian countries.
India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992, a Dialogue Partner in 1996, and a Summit-level Partner in 2002.
The ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) was established in 2010 for goods, and later expanded to services and investment.
India's 'Act East Policy' is a cornerstone of its engagement with ASEAN countries.
The review aims to make the FTA more user-friendly, simple, and trade-facilitative.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of India's engagement with ASEAN through the revised FTA.
Discuss how the updated FTA can help address trade imbalances and promote India's manufacturing exports to Southeast Asia.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for Indian businesses arising from enhanced trade liberalization with ASEAN.
Evaluate the role of the Act East Policy in strengthening India's geopolitical and economic ties in the Indo-Pacific region.
How does the FTA review contribute to regional supply chain resilience and integration?
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed together during a summit meeting.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was founded on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration) by its five founding members: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.

articles.headline: Government Launches Comprehensive National Adaptation Plan for Climate Change
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change unveiled a new National Adaptation Plan (NAP) to enhance India's resilience to climate change impacts.
• The plan integrates sector-specific strategies for agriculture, water resources, health, and coastal areas.
• It emphasizes community-based adaptation, technological solutions, and robust financial mechanisms.
• The NAP aims to align India's adaptation efforts with international commitments under the Paris Agreement.
• Focuses on building capacity at state and local levels to implement adaptation measures effectively.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Climate Change.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: India submitted its first National Adaptation Plan to UNFCCC as part of its commitments.
NAPCC (National Action Plan on Climate Change) launched in 2008 had eight missions.
The new NAP focuses on strengthening adaptation across key vulnerable sectors.
Funding for adaptation often comes from domestic budgets, international climate funds, and green bonds.
Adaptation measures include climate-resilient agriculture, water conservation, and early warning systems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for a comprehensive National Adaptation Plan in India given its vulnerability to climate change impacts.
Analyze the key components of the new NAP and its potential effectiveness in building climate resilience across sectors.
Evaluate the challenges in financing and implementing adaptation strategies at the local and state levels.
Examine the role of technology and traditional knowledge in developing sustainable adaptation solutions.
How does India's adaptation strategy contribute to its broader climate action goals and international commitments?
articles.imageDescription: A visual representation of various climate adaptation strategies like rainwater harvesting, resilient crops, and coastal protection.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases globally, but its per capita emissions are significantly lower than the global average.

articles.headline: ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 Venus Orbiter Mission Makes Significant Progress
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced successful completion of critical orbital maneuvers for its Shukrayaan-1 mission.
• The Venus orbiter is now positioned for detailed study of Venus's atmosphere and surface.
• Scientific instruments on board have begun collecting data, focusing on atmospheric composition, dynamics, and surface features.
• This mission aims to provide unprecedented insights into Venus's geological and atmospheric evolution.
• Shukrayaan-1 solidifies India's growing capabilities in interplanetary space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Shukrayaan-1 is India's first mission to Venus.
The mission aims to study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface using various scientific payloads.
Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to similar size and mass, but has a very different atmosphere.
ISRO is the national space agency of India.
Previous successful ISRO missions include Chandrayaan (Moon) and Mangalyaan (Mars).
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and potential discoveries of ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 mission to Venus.
Analyze the significance of interplanetary missions for advancing scientific knowledge and technological capabilities.
Evaluate India's growing role in global space exploration and its contribution to planetary science.
Examine the challenges involved in designing and executing missions to Venus, given its extreme environment.
How does the success of such missions enhance India's soft power and inspire scientific temper among youth?
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 orbiter circling Venus, with its scientific instruments deployed.
articles.trivia: Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most planets in our solar system, a phenomenon known as retrograde rotation.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Cyber Security Infrastructure Amidst Rising Digital Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced new initiatives to strengthen India's cyber security framework.
• This includes enhanced funding for CERT-In, establishment of new regional cyber security centers, and skill development programs.
• The move comes in response to a surge in cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure and government entities.
• Focus is on developing indigenous cyber security solutions and fostering public-private collaboration.
• Aim is to protect digital assets, ensure data integrity, and build a resilient cyber ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for responding to cyber security incidents in India.
The National Cyber Security Policy 2013 aims to build a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets vital for national security, economy, and public health.
The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) protects CII.
Common cyber threats include phishing, ransomware, malware, and data breaches.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the effectiveness of current cyber security policies and initiatives in protecting critical infrastructure and citizen data.
Examine the challenges in building a robust cyber security ecosystem, including skill gaps, technological advancements, and international cooperation.
Suggest measures for strengthening India's cyber resilience, including legal frameworks, technological upgrades, and public awareness campaigns.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships in countering sophisticated cyberattacks.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized digital network grid with security icons and a lock symbol, representing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: The world's first computer virus, 'Creeper', was created in 1971, designed to move between computers on ARPANET, the precursor to the internet.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Scheme Expands Coverage, Targets Universal Health Access by 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry announced significant progress in expanding the scope and reach of the Ayushman Bharat scheme.
• New initiatives aim to include more vulnerable populations and enhance the network of empanelled hospitals.
• Focus is on strengthening primary healthcare through Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres (AB-HWCs).
• The government reiterated its commitment to achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC) targets by 2030.
• Challenges remain in equitable access, quality of care, and public health infrastructure in remote areas.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
keyTerms: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres (AB-HWCs)
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat scheme was launched in 2018.
It consists of two components: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) and Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs).
PMJAY provides a health cover of Rs 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
HWCs aim to provide Comprehensive Primary Health Care (CPHC) closer to the community.
It is the world's largest government-funded health assurance scheme.
mainsPoints: Assess the impact of the Ayushman Bharat scheme on improving healthcare access and reducing out-of-pocket expenditure for vulnerable populations.
Discuss the challenges in achieving Universal Health Coverage in India, including infrastructure gaps, human resource shortages, and quality of care.
Analyze the role of Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres in strengthening primary healthcare and preventive services.
Evaluate the effectiveness of public-private partnerships in expanding healthcare delivery under the scheme.
Suggest policy reforms needed to ensure equitable and sustainable health services across all regions of India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the Ayushman Bharat logo with images of doctors and patients, symbolizing healthcare access.
articles.trivia: India's National Health Policy 2017 aims to achieve the highest possible level of health and well-being for all through a preventive and promotive healthcare orientation.

articles.headline: Government Expands PLI Scheme to Boost Advanced Manufacturing and R&D
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme has been further expanded to include new sectors critical for advanced manufacturing.
• The expansion aims to incentivize domestic production, attract foreign investment, and promote R&D in high-tech areas.
• New sectors brought under the ambit include advanced materials, specialized chemicals, and certain medical devices.
• This move is expected to enhance India's manufacturing capabilities and reduce reliance on imports.
• The government projects significant job creation and economic growth through these expanded incentives.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme was introduced in 2020 to boost domestic manufacturing and make India a global manufacturing hub.
It offers incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
Initially covered 14 key sectors, including electronics, automobiles, pharmaceuticals, and textiles.
Aims to achieve 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India) by reducing import dependence.
The scheme encourages companies to set up or expand manufacturing units in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the expansion of the PLI scheme and its potential impact on India's manufacturing sector.
Discuss how the PLI scheme can foster innovation and R&D in advanced manufacturing domains.
Evaluate the challenges in attracting large-scale investments and ensuring equitable growth across different sectors under PLI.
Examine the scheme's contribution to job creation, export promotion, and overall economic resilience.
Suggest measures to further enhance the effectiveness and sustainability of the PLI scheme in the long run.
articles.imageDescription: A factory floor with robotic arms and advanced machinery, symbolizing modern manufacturing.
articles.trivia: The 'Make in India' initiative, launched in 2014, aims to encourage companies to manufacture their products in India and incentivize investments into manufacturing.

articles.headline: States Advocate for Greater Fiscal Autonomy and Review of Federal Transfers
articles.explainer:
• Several state governments have collectively called for a comprehensive review of the current fiscal transfer mechanisms from the Centre.
• They argue for greater autonomy in financial decision-making and a larger share in centrally collected taxes.
• The demand stems from increasing responsibilities on states for welfare schemes and infrastructure development.
• Discussions are ongoing with the Finance Commission and the Union Finance Ministry to address these concerns.
• This debate highlights the evolving dynamics of fiscal federalism in India.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government Budgeting.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: The Finance Commission is a constitutional body (Article 280) that recommends the distribution of tax revenues between the Centre and states.
It also recommends the principles governing grants-in-aid to states by the Centre.
Fiscal federalism refers to the division of financial powers and responsibilities between different levels of government.
GST (Goods and Services Tax) subsumed many indirect taxes, impacting state revenues and requiring compensation mechanisms.
Centrally Sponsored Schemes (CSS) are implemented by states but largely funded by the Centre.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges to fiscal federalism in India, particularly concerning the devolution of powers and finances to states.
Analyze the arguments made by states for greater fiscal autonomy and a larger share in central tax revenues.
Evaluate the role of the Finance Commission in mediating fiscal relations between the Centre and states.
Examine the impact of GST implementation and centrally sponsored schemes on states' fiscal space and financial independence.
Suggest reforms to strengthen cooperative federalism and ensure equitable distribution of resources and responsibilities.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a balance scale with 'Centre' and 'States' on either side, representing fiscal federalism.
articles.trivia: The 15th Finance Commission, chaired by N.K. Singh, submitted its report for the period 2021-26, recommending a 41% share of the divisible pool of taxes for states.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Commitment to Global Food Security at UN Summit
articles.explainer:
• India participated actively in a high-level UN summit on food security, emphasizing sustainable agriculture and equitable food distribution.
• The Indian delegation highlighted its successful domestic food security programs and offered expertise to developing nations.
• India called for urgent international cooperation to address challenges posed by climate change, conflicts, and supply chain disruptions.
• Discussions focused on innovative solutions, including climate-resilient crops and improved agricultural practices.
• India advocated for reforms in global food governance and fair trade practices to ensure food access for all.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Issues relating to poverty and hunger.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: SDG 2 aims to 'End hunger, achieve food security and improved nutrition and promote sustainable agriculture'.
India's National Food Security Act (NFSA), 2013, provides legal entitlement to food for a large portion of the population.
The Green Revolution significantly boosted India's food grain production.
Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations that leads international efforts to defeat hunger.
Global food security is impacted by climate change, geopolitical conflicts, and economic instability.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's role and contributions to global food security, both domestically and internationally.
Analyze the multi-faceted challenges to achieving food security worldwide, including climate change, conflicts, and economic disparities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's domestic food security programs, such as PDS and various agricultural schemes.
Examine the need for international cooperation and reforms in global food governance to ensure equitable access to food.
Suggest strategies for promoting sustainable and climate-resilient agriculture to address future food demands.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized globe surrounded by images of diverse crops and people, symbolizing global food security.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest producers of several food items, including milk, pulses, and spices.

articles.headline: Preliminary Findings of Tiger Census 2025 Show Encouraging Trends
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change released preliminary findings of the 2025 All India Tiger Estimation.
• Initial data suggests a continued increase in India's tiger population, reinforcing conservation efforts.
• The census employed advanced camera-trapping and AI-based image analysis technologies for accuracy.
• Emphasis was placed on habitat connectivity, prey base management, and anti-poaching measures.
• The final report is expected by early 2026, providing detailed insights into tiger distribution and population dynamics.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: India conducts an All India Tiger Estimation every four years.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to conserve tigers and their habitats.
National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
Tiger reserves are legally protected areas for tiger conservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the All India Tiger Estimation in monitoring tiger populations and informing conservation strategies.
Analyze the factors contributing to the increase in India's tiger population, including Project Tiger and community involvement.
Evaluate the ongoing challenges in tiger conservation, such as habitat loss, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching.
Examine the role of technology (e.g., camera traps, AI) in enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of wildlife census.
Suggest measures for strengthening tiger corridors and ensuring the long-term viability of tiger habitats.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage, captured by a camera trap.
articles.trivia: Tigers are solitary animals, and each tiger's stripe pattern is unique, much like human fingerprints.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Quantum Computing Research
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at a premier Indian institute announced a significant advancement in quantum computing, developing a more stable qubit architecture.
• The breakthrough addresses a critical challenge in quantum computing: maintaining qubit coherence for longer durations.
• This development could accelerate the path towards building fault-tolerant quantum computers.
• The research leveraged indigenous expertise and advanced material science to achieve the stable state.
• It positions India as a key player in the global race for quantum technology dominance.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Computers, Robotics, Nano-technology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights.
prelimsPoints: Quantum computing uses principles of quantum mechanics (superposition, entanglement) to solve complex problems faster than classical computers.
A qubit is the basic unit of information in quantum computing, analogous to a bit in classical computing.
Quantum coherence refers to the ability of a quantum system to maintain its quantum properties.
India launched the National Quantum Mission (NQM) in 2023 to foster R&D in quantum technologies.
Applications of quantum computing include drug discovery, materials science, financial modeling, and cryptography.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of quantum computing and its potential to revolutionize various sectors, including healthcare, finance, and defense.
Analyze the challenges in developing practical quantum computers, particularly concerning qubit stability and error correction.
Evaluate India's strategic initiatives, such as the National Quantum Mission, in fostering indigenous research and development in quantum technology.
Examine the ethical and security implications of advancements in quantum computing, including its impact on cryptography.
Suggest measures to strengthen India's quantum ecosystem, including talent development, infrastructure investment, and international collaborations.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic representing qubits in a quantum computer, with intricate connections and glowing nodes.
articles.trivia: The concept of quantum computing was first proposed by physicist Richard Feynman in the early 1980s.

articles.headline: Editorial: The Imperative of Balancing Economic Growth with Environmental Sustainability
articles.explainer:
• The editorial argues that India's rapid economic growth must be carefully balanced with robust environmental protection measures.
• It highlights the increasing frequency of extreme weather events and resource depletion as critical challenges.
• Calls for a paradigm shift towards green growth models, circular economy principles, and sustainable infrastructure development.
• Emphasizes the need for stronger regulatory frameworks and effective implementation of environmental policies.
• Suggests that long-term prosperity is inextricably linked to ecological health and climate resilience.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the inherent conflict and potential synergy between rapid economic growth and environmental sustainability in India's development trajectory.
Analyze the various environmental challenges faced by India, including pollution, resource depletion, and climate change impacts.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government policies and regulations in promoting sustainable development and environmental protection.
Examine the concept of 'green growth' and 'circular economy' as viable models for India's future economic development.
Suggest policy recommendations and societal shifts required to achieve a harmonious balance between economic progress and ecological preservation.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a tree growing out of a gear, symbolizing sustainable economic development.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'sustainable development' was popularized by the Brundtland Commission Report (Our Common Future) in 1987, defining it as development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.

articles.headline: India's First Fully Private Sector Rocket Successfully Launched from Sriharikota
articles.explainer:
• A significant milestone was achieved as India's first privately developed rocket, 'Agnibaan SOrTeD', successfully completed its sub-orbital test flight.
• Developed by Agnikul Cosmos, the launch demonstrated the growing capabilities of India's private space sector.
• The mission aimed to test the company's patented Agnilet engine, the world's first single-piece 3D-printed rocket engine.
• This success opens new avenues for cost-effective access to space and boosts India's space economy.
• ISRO provided crucial support and guidance, fostering a vibrant ecosystem for space startups.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Agnikul Cosmos is an Indian space tech startup based in Chennai.
Agnibaan SOrTeD (SubOrbital Test Flight) is a single-stage rocket for sub-orbital flight.
Agnilet is the world's first single-piece 3D-printed engine designed for rocket propulsion.
IN-SPACe (Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre) facilitates private sector participation in space activities.
This launch marks a significant step towards India becoming a global space hub.
mainsPoints: Discuss the burgeoning role of the private sector in India's space industry and its implications for national development.
Analyze how private participation can enhance India's capabilities in space exploration, satellite launch, and space-based services.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for space startups in India, including funding, regulatory support, and technological innovation.
Examine the potential economic benefits of a thriving private space sector, such as job creation, technological spin-offs, and export opportunities.
How is ISRO fostering collaboration with the private sector to accelerate India's space program?
articles.imageDescription: A sleek, small rocket lifting off from a launchpad with a trail of smoke and fire.
articles.trivia: India's space economy is projected to reach $50 billion by 2025, driven by both public and private sector contributions.

articles.headline: Government Launches Mega Digital Literacy Mission for Rural India
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Rural Development launched a new, ambitious Digital Literacy Mission aimed at empowering rural populations with essential digital skills.
• The mission targets millions of rural households, focusing on women and marginalized communities, to bridge the digital divide.
• Training modules cover basic computer operations, internet usage, online banking, and government digital services.
• The initiative leverages local community centers, self-help groups, and volunteer networks for widespread outreach.
• Expected to boost digital inclusion, facilitate access to online services, and enhance economic opportunities in rural areas.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
keyTerms: Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA)
prelimsPoints: PMGDISHA (Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan) was a previous scheme for digital literacy in rural areas.
The new mission builds upon previous efforts to achieve universal digital literacy.
Aims to make at least one person digitally literate in every eligible rural household.
Digital literacy is crucial for accessing government services, financial services, and educational content online.
Common Service Centres (CSCs) play a vital role in delivering digital services in rural areas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of digital literacy in fostering inclusive growth and bridging the digital divide in India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing digital literacy programs in rural areas, including infrastructure, language barriers, and cultural resistance.
Evaluate the potential impact of the new Digital Literacy Mission on rural empowerment, economic opportunities, and access to governance services.
Examine the role of technology and community participation in ensuring the success and sustainability of such initiatives.
Suggest measures to further enhance digital inclusion and address the unique needs of diverse rural populations.
articles.imageDescription: A group of rural women learning to use tablets and computers in a community center, with a digital literacy instructor.
articles.trivia: India has the world's second-largest internet user base, yet a significant portion of its rural population still lacks basic digital skills.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover Multiple New Species in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A team of researchers announced the discovery of several new species of flora and fauna in the Western Ghats.
• The findings include new amphibians, insects, and plant varieties, highlighting the region's rich biodiversity.
• The discoveries underscore the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts in this ecologically fragile area.
• Genetic analysis and field surveys were crucial in identifying these previously unrecorded species.
• This emphasizes the Western Ghats' status as a global biodiversity hotspot and its importance for ecological balance.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots.
It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for its high levels of endemism.
Endemic species are those found only in a specific geographical area.
The region is home to numerous national parks, wildlife sanctuaries, and tiger reserves.
Threats to the Western Ghats include deforestation, mining, climate change, and human encroachment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the implications of new species discoveries.
Analyze the various threats facing the Western Ghats ecosystem and their impact on biodiversity.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current conservation policies and protected area networks in safeguarding the region's flora and fauna.
Examine the role of scientific research and public awareness in driving conservation efforts in such critical ecological zones.
Suggest integrated approaches for sustainable development and biodiversity conservation in the Western Ghats region.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a newly discovered colorful frog species found in the Western Ghats rainforest.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and influence India's monsoon weather patterns.

articles.headline: India Strengthens Maritime Security Strategy in the Indo-Pacific Region
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy announced significant upgrades to its maritime security strategy, focusing on the Indo-Pacific region.
• This includes enhanced naval exercises with partner nations, increased surveillance capabilities, and capacity building initiatives.
• The move aims to counter rising geopolitical complexities and ensure freedom of navigation in critical sea lanes.
• India reiterated its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning with Quad objectives.
• Focus is also on bolstering domestic shipbuilding and naval modernization programs.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Security challenges and their management in border areas; linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: The Indo-Pacific concept refers to the interconnectedness of the Indian and Pacific Oceans, vital for global trade.
Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) comprises India, USA, Japan, and Australia, focusing on a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Indian Navy is the fifth-largest in the world, undergoing significant modernization.
Key challenges in the Indo-Pacific include territorial disputes, piracy, and freedom of navigation concerns.
India's SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) doctrine emphasizes maritime cooperation with littoral states.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the Indo-Pacific region for India's security and economic interests.
Discuss the key components of India's strengthened maritime security strategy and its implications for regional stability.
Evaluate the role of international collaborations, such as Quad, in promoting a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Examine the challenges faced by India in maintaining maritime security, including geopolitical rivalries and non-traditional threats.
Suggest measures for enhancing India's naval capabilities and strengthening its position as a net security provider in the region.
articles.imageDescription: Indian naval warships conducting a joint exercise in the open sea, with a helicopter flying overhead.
articles.trivia: India's coastline spans over 7,500 kilometers, making maritime security crucial for its trade and strategic interests.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Promote Millet Cultivation and Consumption
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare launched a comprehensive policy aimed at boosting millet production and popularizing their consumption.
• The policy includes incentives for farmers, research into high-yielding varieties, and integration into public food programs.
• Millets are promoted as 'nutri-cereals' for their high nutritional value, climate resilience, and low water footprint.
• The initiative aligns with India's commitment to food security, farmer income enhancement, and sustainable agriculture.
• Awareness campaigns will target consumers, highlighting the health benefits and versatility of millets.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
articles.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
prelimsPoints: Millets are a group of highly variable small-seeded grasses, widely grown around the world as cereal crops.
Common millets include Jowar (sorghum), Bajra (pearl millet), Ragi (finger millet).
Millets are known for being drought-resistant and requiring less water compared to rice and wheat.
The United Nations declared 2023 as the International Year of Millets.
They are rich in fiber, protein, and essential minerals, offering significant nutritional benefits.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional benefits of promoting millet cultivation and consumption for India's agriculture, nutrition, and environment.
Analyze the challenges faced by millet farmers and the strategies outlined in the new policy to address them.
Evaluate the role of millets in achieving food and nutritional security, especially in the context of climate change.
Examine the potential for millets to diversify India's cropping patterns and reduce dependence on water-intensive crops.
Suggest measures to increase consumer awareness and integrate millets into mainstream diets and food processing industries.
articles.imageDescription: A variety of millets displayed in bowls, showcasing their diversity and nutritional appeal.
articles.trivia: Millets were among the first crops to be domesticated by humans and are considered 'ancient grains'.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Witnesses Post-Pandemic Boom, Focus on Sustainable Practices
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism reported a significant surge in both domestic and international tourist arrivals, surpassing pre-pandemic levels.
• The revival is attributed to government initiatives, improved connectivity, and a focus on diverse tourism offerings.
• Emphasis is now being placed on promoting sustainable and responsible tourism practices across destinations.
• New policies aim to enhance infrastructure, promote local cultures, and ensure environmental protection.
• The sector is poised for continued growth, contributing significantly to employment and foreign exchange earnings.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Tourism sector and its impact on economy and employment.
prelimsPoints: Tourism is a major contributor to India's GDP and employment.
India offers diverse tourism experiences: cultural, adventure, medical, spiritual, and ecotourism.
The 'Incredible India' campaign promotes India as a global tourist destination.
Sustainable tourism aims to minimize negative impacts and maximize benefits for local communities and environment.
The Ministry of Tourism is the nodal agency for tourism development in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the recent resurgence of India's tourism sector and its economic significance in terms of employment and foreign exchange.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in promoting sustainable and responsible tourism practices across diverse destinations in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives and policies in boosting tourism infrastructure and attracting both domestic and international tourists.
Examine the role of tourism in preserving cultural heritage, promoting local economies, and fostering cross-cultural understanding.
Suggest measures to ensure long-term, inclusive, and environmentally friendly growth of India's tourism industry.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant collage of famous Indian tourist destinations, including historical monuments, beaches, and mountains.
articles.trivia: The Taj Mahal, one of the New Seven Wonders of the World, is India's most visited tourist attraction.

articles.headline: Global Chip Shortage Continues to Impact Indian Industries, Government Pushes Domestic Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• The persistent global semiconductor chip shortage continues to disrupt various Indian industries, particularly automotive and electronics.
• Supply chain bottlenecks and geopolitical factors are exacerbating the scarcity of critical components.
• The government reiterated its commitment to the Semiconductor Mission, aiming to establish indigenous chip manufacturing capabilities.
• Incentives are being offered to attract global players and foster a domestic ecosystem for semiconductor design and fabrication.
• The long-term strategy focuses on achieving self-reliance in this strategically vital sector.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Semiconductor chips are essential components for modern electronics, from smartphones to cars.
The global chip shortage began in 2020 due to various factors including increased demand, supply chain disruptions, and geopolitical tensions.
India's Semiconductor Mission aims to build a robust semiconductor and display manufacturing ecosystem.
PLI scheme for semiconductors and display manufacturing offers fiscal incentives.
India currently imports a significant portion of its semiconductor requirements.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the global chip shortage on India's industrial sector and economic growth.
Analyze the strategic importance of developing indigenous semiconductor manufacturing capabilities for India's technological sovereignty and national security.
Evaluate the government's initiatives, such as the Semiconductor Mission and PLI scheme, in attracting investments and fostering a domestic chip ecosystem.
Examine the challenges in establishing semiconductor fabrication units in India, including high capital costs, technology transfer, and skilled workforce availability.
Suggest a comprehensive roadmap for India to achieve self-reliance in semiconductor manufacturing and design.
articles.imageDescription: Close-up of a complex semiconductor chip with intricate circuitry, symbolizing advanced technology.
articles.trivia: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is the world's largest dedicated independent semiconductor foundry, producing chips for many global tech giants.

highlights: India launches Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting global leadership in clean energy production.
11th International Yoga Day celebrated worldwide with the theme 'Yoga for Self and Society', promoting holistic well-being.
Supreme Court upholds key provisions of India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, bringing clarity to data privacy laws.
India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement review concludes, set to boost trade and investment in the Indo-Pacific region.
India's first fully private sector rocket, 'Agnibaan SOrTeD' by Agnikul Cosmos, successfully completes its sub-orbital test flight.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) is/are correct?
1. It aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. The SIGHT program under NGHM supports domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
3. India aims to produce 10 MMT of green hydrogen by 2030.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NGHM aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen. Statement 2 is correct: The SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) program is a key component supporting domestic manufacturing and production. Statement 3 is incorrect: The target is to produce 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) of green hydrogen by 2030, not 10 MMT.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding International Yoga Day:
1. It is celebrated annually on June 21st.
2. The United Nations General Assembly adopted the resolution to observe Yoga Day in 2014.
3. The theme for International Yoga Day 2025 is 'Yoga for Climate Action'.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: International Yoga Day is celebrated on June 21st. Statement 2 is correct: The UNGA adopted the resolution in December 2014, leading to the first celebration in 2015. Statement 3 is incorrect: The theme for International Yoga Day 2025 is 'Yoga for Self and Society', not 'Yoga for Climate Action'.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, consider the following statements:
1. It recognizes the 'right to privacy' as a fundamental right.
2. It establishes the Data Protection Board of India for enforcement.
3. It applies only to the processing of personal data within India.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Act is based on the recognition of the 'right to privacy' as a fundamental right (K.S. Puttaswamy judgment). Statement 2 is correct: The Data Protection Board of India is the enforcement body. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data within India and also to certain offshore processing activities if they relate to offering goods or services to data principals in India.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is a constitutional body responsible for recommending the distribution of tax revenues between the Union and the States in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Finance Commission is a constitutional body established under Article 280 of the Indian Constitution. Its primary role is to make recommendations to the President of India on the distribution of net proceeds of taxes between the Union and the States, and also on the principles governing grants-in-aid to the States by the Centre.

prelimsMocks.question: What is 'Agnilet', recently in news?
options: (a) A new indigenous fighter jet engine developed by DRDO.
(b) The world's first single-piece 3D-printed rocket engine.
(c) A specialized satellite for climate change monitoring.
(d) India's new anti-tank guided missile system.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) The world's first single-piece 3D-printed rocket engine.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Agnilet is the world's first single-piece 3D-printed rocket engine, developed by the Indian space tech startup Agnikul Cosmos. It was successfully tested as part of the Agnibaan SOrTeD sub-orbital test flight, marking a significant milestone for India's private space sector.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving India's energy security and climate goals. What challenges must India overcome to become a global leader in green hydrogen production?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Energy; Infrastructure; Environmental pollution and degradation; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NGHM and its objectives. Explain its importance for energy security (reduced fossil fuel imports) and climate goals (decarbonization, NDCs). Detail the economic and environmental benefits. Outline key challenges such as high production costs, technology scaling, infrastructure development, and policy support. Conclude with a forward-looking statement on India's potential.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the evolving dynamics of fiscal federalism in India, especially in the context of states' demands for greater fiscal autonomy. What reforms are needed to strengthen cooperative federalism and ensure equitable resource distribution?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein; GS3: Government Budgeting.
mainsMocks.approach: Define fiscal federalism and its constitutional basis. Discuss the reasons for states' demands (e.g., increased responsibilities, GST impact, centrally sponsored schemes). Analyze the role of the Finance Commission. Propose reforms such as increasing states' share in central taxes, rationalizing CSS, enhancing states' borrowing limits, and strengthening local bodies' finances. Conclude by emphasizing the importance of trust and cooperation.

mainsMocks.question: Evaluate India's efforts in strengthening its cyber security infrastructure amidst rising digital threats. What are the key challenges in building a robust cyber ecosystem, and what measures can be adopted for enhanced cyber resilience?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the increasing cyber threat landscape and its impact on India. Discuss current efforts (CERT-In, NCIIPC, policies, skill development). Detail challenges like evolving threats, skill gap, lack of awareness, cross-border attacks, and legal complexities. Suggest measures for enhanced resilience: robust legal framework, international cooperation, R&D in indigenous tech, public-private partnerships, and mass awareness campaigns. Conclude with the vision of a secure digital India.

quickBytes.text: June 21st marks the Summer Solstice in the Northern Hemisphere, the longest day of the year.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 5 MMT production by 2030, reducing fossil fuel imports.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is enforced by the Data Protection Board of India.
ASEAN was founded in 1967 by Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.
Shukrayaan-1 is ISRO's first mission to study Venus's atmosphere and surface.
Ayushman Bharat PMJAY provides health cover of ₹5 lakh per family for secondary and tertiary care.
The PLI scheme incentives domestic manufacturing based on incremental sales in identified sectors.
The Finance Commission is a constitutional body (Article 280) for Centre-State fiscal transfers.
India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, as per recent estimations.
Agnikul Cosmos's 'Agnibaan SOrTeD' used Agnilet, the world's first 3D-printed rocket engine.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Scheme Expands Coverage, Targets Universal Health Access by 2030
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry announced significant progress in expanding the scope and reach of the Ayushman Bharat scheme.
• New initiatives aim to include more vulnerable populations and enhance the network of empanelled hospitals.
• Focus is on strengthening primary healthcare through Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres (AB-HWCs).
• The government reiterated its commitment to achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC) targets by 2030.
• Challenges remain in equitable access, quality of care, and public health infrastructure in remote areas.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
keyTerms: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres (AB-HWCs)
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat scheme was launched in 2018.
It consists of two components: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) and Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs).
PMJAY provides a health cover of Rs 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
HWCs aim to provide Comprehensive Primary Health Care (CPHC) closer to the community.
It is the world's largest government-funded health assurance scheme.
mainsPoints: Assess the impact of the Ayushman Bharat scheme on improving healthcare access and reducing out-of-pocket expenditure for vulnerable populations.
Discuss the challenges in achieving Universal Health Coverage in India, including infrastructure gaps, human resource shortages, and quality of care.
Analyze the role of Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres in strengthening primary healthcare and preventive services.
Evaluate the effectiveness of public-private partnerships in expanding healthcare delivery under the scheme.
Suggest policy reforms needed to ensure equitable and sustainable health services across all regions of India.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the Ayushman Bharat logo with images of doctors and patients, symbolizing healthcare access.
GS2.trivia: India's National Health Policy 2017 aims to achieve the highest possible level of health and well-being for all through a preventive and promotive healthcare orientation.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targeting Global Leadership
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and developing export infrastructure.
• Phase II aims to further incentivize domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and boost green hydrogen production capacity.
• The mission seeks to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production, utilization, and export.
• Emphasizes public-private partnerships and research & development to reduce production costs.
• This phase is crucial for achieving India's energy independence and climate targets by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Energy; Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) launched in 2023.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Phase II focuses on scaling up production and developing export infrastructure.
Target to produce 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) of green hydrogen by 2030.
SIGHT program (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) supports domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and production of green hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's energy security strategy through green hydrogen.
Analyze the economic potential for job creation, industrial growth, and export revenue.
Examine the environmental benefits in reducing carbon emissions and achieving climate targets.
Evaluate challenges like high initial costs, technology scaling, and infrastructure development.
Role of NGHM in Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative and global energy transition.
GS3.imageDescription: An artist's rendition of a large-scale green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making its production process carbon-free and sustainable.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest meeting.
• The decision was influenced by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food and energy sectors.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to bringing inflation within the target band while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the central bank, with future rate adjustments dependent on inflation trajectory.
• The MPC also highlighted global economic uncertainties and geopolitical developments impacting domestic inflation outlook.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy and its instruments.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
The primary objective of monetary policy in India is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2% (2% to 6%).
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Reverse repo rate is the rate at which the RBI borrows money from commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global scenario.
Analyze the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflationary pressures in India.
Examine the impact of global geopolitical events and supply chain disruptions on India's domestic inflation.
Evaluate the role of the MPC in ensuring transparency and accountability in monetary policy decisions.
Suggest alternative or complementary measures to monetary policy for sustainable economic stability.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai with its distinctive logo, under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: The first Governor of the Reserve Bank of India was Sir Osborne Smith, while the first Indian Governor was C.D. Deshmukh.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Comprehensive National Adaptation Plan for Climate Change
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change unveiled a new National Adaptation Plan (NAP) to enhance India's resilience to climate change impacts.
• The plan integrates sector-specific strategies for agriculture, water resources, health, and coastal areas.
• It emphasizes community-based adaptation, technological solutions, and robust financial mechanisms.
• The NAP aims to align India's adaptation efforts with international commitments under the Paris Agreement.
• Focuses on building capacity at state and local levels to implement adaptation measures effectively.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Climate Change.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: India submitted its first National Adaptation Plan to UNFCCC as part of its commitments.
NAPCC (National Action Plan on Climate Change) launched in 2008 had eight missions.
The new NAP focuses on strengthening adaptation across key vulnerable sectors.
Funding for adaptation often comes from domestic budgets, international climate funds, and green bonds.
Adaptation measures include climate-resilient agriculture, water conservation, and early warning systems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for a comprehensive National Adaptation Plan in India given its vulnerability to climate change impacts.
Analyze the key components of the new NAP and its potential effectiveness in building climate resilience across sectors.
Evaluate the challenges in financing and implementing adaptation strategies at the local and state levels.
Examine the role of technology and traditional knowledge in developing sustainable adaptation solutions.
How does India's adaptation strategy contribute to its broader climate action goals and international commitments?
GS3.imageDescription: A visual representation of various climate adaptation strategies like rainwater harvesting, resilient crops, and coastal protection.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases globally, but its per capita emissions are significantly lower than the global average.

GS3.headline: ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 Venus Orbiter Mission Makes Significant Progress
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced successful completion of critical orbital maneuvers for its Shukrayaan-1 mission.
• The Venus orbiter is now positioned for detailed study of Venus's atmosphere and surface.
• Scientific instruments on board have begun collecting data, focusing on atmospheric composition, dynamics, and surface features.
• This mission aims to provide unprecedented insights into Venus's geological and atmospheric evolution.
• Shukrayaan-1 solidifies India's growing capabilities in interplanetary space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Shukrayaan-1 is India's first mission to Venus.
The mission aims to study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface using various scientific payloads.
Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to similar size and mass, but has a very different atmosphere.
ISRO is the national space agency of India.
Previous successful ISRO missions include Chandrayaan (Moon) and Mangalyaan (Mars).
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and potential discoveries of ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 mission to Venus.
Analyze the significance of interplanetary missions for advancing scientific knowledge and technological capabilities.
Evaluate India's growing role in global space exploration and its contribution to planetary science.
Examine the challenges involved in designing and executing missions to Venus, given its extreme environment.
How does the success of such missions enhance India's soft power and inspire scientific temper among youth?
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 orbiter circling Venus, with its scientific instruments deployed.
GS3.trivia: Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most planets in our solar system, a phenomenon known as retrograde rotation.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Cyber Security Infrastructure Amidst Rising Digital Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced new initiatives to strengthen India's cyber security framework.
• This includes enhanced funding for CERT-In, establishment of new regional cyber security centers, and skill development programs.
• The move comes in response to a surge in cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure and government entities.
• Focus is on developing indigenous cyber security solutions and fostering public-private collaboration.
• Aim is to protect digital assets, ensure data integrity, and build a resilient cyber ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for responding to cyber security incidents in India.
The National Cyber Security Policy 2013 aims to build a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets vital for national security, economy, and public health.
The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) protects CII.
Common cyber threats include phishing, ransomware, malware, and data breaches.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the effectiveness of current cyber security policies and initiatives in protecting critical infrastructure and citizen data.
Examine the challenges in building a robust cyber security ecosystem, including skill gaps, technological advancements, and international cooperation.
Suggest measures for strengthening India's cyber resilience, including legal frameworks, technological upgrades, and public awareness campaigns.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships in countering sophisticated cyberattacks.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized digital network grid with security icons and a lock symbol, representing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: The world's first computer virus, 'Creeper', was created in 1971, designed to move between computers on ARPANET, the precursor to the internet.

GS3.headline: Government Expands PLI Scheme to Boost Advanced Manufacturing and R&D
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme has been further expanded to include new sectors critical for advanced manufacturing.
• The expansion aims to incentivize domestic production, attract foreign investment, and promote R&D in high-tech areas.
• New sectors brought under the ambit include advanced materials, specialized chemicals, and certain medical devices.
• This move is expected to enhance India's manufacturing capabilities and reduce reliance on imports.
• The government projects significant job creation and economic growth through these expanded incentives.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme was introduced in 2020 to boost domestic manufacturing and make India a global manufacturing hub.
It offers incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
Initially covered 14 key sectors, including electronics, automobiles, pharmaceuticals, and textiles.
Aims to achieve 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India) by reducing import dependence.
The scheme encourages companies to set up or expand manufacturing units in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the expansion of the PLI scheme and its potential impact on India's manufacturing sector.
Discuss how the PLI scheme can foster innovation and R&D in advanced manufacturing domains.
Evaluate the challenges in attracting large-scale investments and ensuring equitable growth across different sectors under PLI.
Examine the scheme's contribution to job creation, export promotion, and overall economic resilience.
Suggest measures to further enhance the effectiveness and sustainability of the PLI scheme in the long run.
GS3.imageDescription: A factory floor with robotic arms and advanced machinery, symbolizing modern manufacturing.
GS3.trivia: The 'Make in India' initiative, launched in 2014, aims to encourage companies to manufacture their products in India and incentivize investments into manufacturing.

GS3.headline: Preliminary Findings of Tiger Census 2025 Show Encouraging Trends
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change released preliminary findings of the 2025 All India Tiger Estimation.
• Initial data suggests a continued increase in India's tiger population, reinforcing conservation efforts.
• The census employed advanced camera-trapping and AI-based image analysis technologies for accuracy.
• Emphasis was placed on habitat connectivity, prey base management, and anti-poaching measures.
• The final report is expected by early 2026, providing detailed insights into tiger distribution and population dynamics.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: India conducts an All India Tiger Estimation every four years.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to conserve tigers and their habitats.
National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
Tiger reserves are legally protected areas for tiger conservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the All India Tiger Estimation in monitoring tiger populations and informing conservation strategies.
Analyze the factors contributing to the increase in India's tiger population, including Project Tiger and community involvement.
Evaluate the ongoing challenges in tiger conservation, such as habitat loss, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching.
Examine the role of technology (e.g., camera traps, AI) in enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of wildlife census.
Suggest measures for strengthening tiger corridors and ensuring the long-term viability of tiger habitats.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage, captured by a camera trap.
GS3.trivia: Tigers are solitary animals, and each tiger's stripe pattern is unique, much like human fingerprints.

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Quantum Computing Research
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers at a premier Indian institute announced a significant advancement in quantum computing, developing a more stable qubit architecture.
• The breakthrough addresses a critical challenge in quantum computing: maintaining qubit coherence for longer durations.
• This development could accelerate the path towards building fault-tolerant quantum computers.
• The research leveraged indigenous expertise and advanced material science to achieve the stable state.
• It positions India as a key player in the global race for quantum technology dominance.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
